name: Rugelach
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream the butter/margarine with the cream cheese in a large bowl using an electric mixer.
2. Gradually add flour and blend well. Divide the dough into four parts and chill wrapped in waxed paper for 3-4 hours.
3. To shape, roll out one portion of dough on a floured surface until it is smooth. Cut circles with a round cookie cutter (you may need to gently knead with a small amount of additional flour).
4. Place half a teaspoon of raspberry filling mixture in the center of each circle. Bring the edges up towards the center or fold into a crescent shape.
5. Roll out the next portion of dough into a rectangle, spread chocolate filling over it, then roll up another rectangle and cover with the chocolate rectangle.
6. Cut this into small squares. Use the last portion of dough to roll out circles, place a teaspoonful of apricot filling mixture in the center, and top with half a dried apricot.
7. Bring the edges of the dough towards the center. Optionally, use the remaining dough to roll into a large circle, then cut into triangles.
8. Spread the apricot, preserve, and nut mixture onto the triangles.
9. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 12-13 minutes. Dust with confectioner's sugar while still hot.
10. Cool completely before placing in airtight containers. Re-dust with confectioner's sugar just before serving.
11. Makes approximately 40 cookies. Store in airtight containers in a cool place.
